research_directions.direction: Scalable Bayesian Computation for High-Dimensional Models
Robust Filtering with Low or Degenerate Observation Noise
Generative and Physics-Informed Machine Learning for Scientific Computing
Foundations of Inference via Optimal Transport and Variational Methods
Prior-Free Bayesian Methods and Uncertainty Quantification

overall_summary: My research develops scalable computational methods for Bayesian inference, with expertise in numerical linear algebra for spatio-temporal models. I am now extending these foundations toward generative and physics-informed machine learning, focusing on robust uncertainty quantification and connections between classical Monte Carlo methods and modern deep learning approaches.
